prompt:
You are the Product Manager agent critiquing a drafted Method artifact, following Juval Lowy's The Method.
Respond with a single JSON object. No prose outside the JSON.
Artifact under review: Mission
Your JSON output MUST conform exactly to this JSON Schema (notes is required when verdict=2/Revise: the revision the architect should make).
Schema: {"properties":{"verdict":{"type":"integer","enum":[1,2]},"notes":{"type":"string"}},"additionalProperties":false,"type":"object","required":["verdict","notes"]}

Draft under review:
{"vision":"A connected community where local events organize themselves around the people who care.","objectives":[{"number":1,"statement":"Sustain organizer retention above 70% quarter-over-quarter by absorbing changes in notification channels, identity providers, and venue data sources without disrupting the organizing experience."},{"number":2,"statement":"Achieve and maintain member RSVP-to-attendance conversion above 60% across all active events, demonstrating that engagement mechanisms reliably translate intent into participation."},{"number":3,"statement":"Onboard new notification channels, identity providers, or venue data integrations within one release cycle and at no more than 15% incremental engineering cost, proving the platform's adaptability to a shifting external ecosystem."}],"mission":"The system encapsulates the volatility of how communities connect, participate, and gather behind a stable core of interacting components. An organizing engine governs the lifecycle of events and their relationships to members, delegating to a pluggable notification dispatcher whose channel implementations evolve independently of the engagement logic it serves. An identity broker abstracts the chosen identity provider so that member and organizer relationships remain constant as the underlying provider changes. A venue resolver mediates between events and external venue data sources, allowing those sources to be swapped without altering the event's structure. These components collaborate so that engagement (RSVP, comment, reminder) is orchestrated centrally while the channels, identities, and venues through which it flows remain interchangeable—enabling the platform to evolve its external relationships continuously from initial draft through deployment."}

Task: as the Product Manager, ratify the draft (Approve) or request a concrete revision (Revise with notes). Ratify only what faithfully serves the business; the human makes the final commit decision.

response.notes: The Mission artifact is approved. The vision is crisp and customer-oriented. All three objectives are measurable, time-bound in cadence, and directly traceable to the volatility axes called out in the mission narrative (notification channels, identity providers, venue data sources). The mission statement correctly encapsulates volatility behind stable components, names the key architectural services without over-specifying implementation, and closes the loop from draft through deployment. Business intent and system intent are coherently aligned.
